items.name: Mano
items.description: The Grandfather of all snails. Mano has no attacks but can cast buffs, slow, and summon mobs of snails. When defeated, it drops a rainbow colored snail shell which can allow a player to transform into a Red Snail. Either Mano is the last remaining Mossy Snail or his shell is so old, weeds and grass have begun to grow over the shell. The best way to find it is to go channel surfing until it appears. Spawns every 45 minutes.
items.shortDescription: The Grandfather of all snails
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Master of Disguise
items.description: A mysterious stranger from the continent of Ereve. Must be defeated as part of a quest for new Cygnus Knights, which Neinheart mentions is used as a test of judgment for newer Cygnus Knights, previously being a part of the 3rd Job Advancement for Cygnus Knights. His main attack is smash which causes a super knock back if it hits you. Good communication is necessary to prevail!
items.shortDescription: A mysterious stranger from the continent of Ereve
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Dyle
items.description: A giant alligator that was accidentally created by Lakelis. It was once a mere Croco, but upon swallowing some Magic Potion of Growth that Lakelis threw into the sewer, it grew to the current size. Roams the swamps near Kerning City, serving as its area boss. It looks as though the bird is tricking the alligator into doing its bidding. It has no magic attacks, but it can cast some buffs, and when this happens the bird smacks the alligator in the head with the stick, as a warning. In addition, it can heal itself and nearby allies by 5,000.
items.shortDescription: A giant alligator that was accidentally created by Lakelis

This monster can send out a burst of spikes to attack whenever you go close to it, but it is immobile. It proves to be relatively harmless to long-ranged characters such as Hermits, Archers, and Magicians, unless they spawn in a swarm around the player.
items.shortDescription: A small sea-urchin like monster found in east Aqua Road
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Eliza

She summon Jr. Lucidas to protect her. Found in Orbis: Garden of Darkness II because the actual Eliza confined her there. The actual Eliza is an NPC for a quest where you have to use a huge Lyre to produce the song "Twinkle Twinkle Little Star".

items.name: Dances with Balrog's Clone
items.description: The dark clone of Dances with Balrog. In order to advance as a Crusader, White Knight, or Dragon Knight, a warrior must challenge this monster when above level 60 and defeat it. Has a powerful attack that only works melee. Wields a glowing version of the level 80 one handed axe Gaea. While it's hard to avoid in battle since a Warrior needs to go close as well, if you need to heal or re-add enchantment, you can just run away from the clone and he won't be able to hurt you at any distance. He is also able to cast Blind and summons Tauromacis.
items.shortDescription: The dark clone of Dances with Balrog
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Grendel the Really Old's Clone
items.description: The dark clone of Grendel the Really Old. In order to advance as a Mage or Priest, a magician must challenge this monster when level 60 or above and defeat it. Uses 3rd Job Mage skills, such as Explosion. Slowest attacking clone and an easy fight for mages, since it cannot teleport, while they can (teleporting at the right moment negates Grendel's attacks). It can also seal and summons Tauromacis.
items.shortDescription: The dark clone of Grendel the Really Old
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Athena Pierce's Clone
items.description: The dark clone of Athena Pierce. In order to advance as a Ranger or Sniper, a bowman must challenge this monster when level 60 or above and defeat it. She is wearing a brown Lineros (level 70 female overall) and wields the famed Dark Arund. The strafe fired by Athena is unusual in that it is only a single powerful arrow. Athena is also able to inflict weakness, which will interfere with jumpshots and evading Athena's attacks. She can also summons Tauromacis.
items.shortDescription: The dark clone of Athena Pierce
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Dark Lord's Clone
items.description: The dark clone of the Dark Lord. In order to advance as a Chief Bandit or Hermit, a thief must challenge this monster when level 60 or above and defeat it. Also uses 3rd Job skills, such as a Hermit's Avenger. Wears a Golden version of the level 60 thief hat Identity, and wields the Dark Scarab and a Dark Stud top and bottom. It can use weaken, which is a disadvantage to assassins, so stay out of it's affliction range. She can also summons Tauromacis.
items.shortDescription: The dark clone of the Dark Lord
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Shadow Kyrin
items.description: The dark clone of Kyrin. In order to make the job advancement as an Infighter, a Pirate must challenge this monster when level 60 or above and defeat it. It uses many attack skills from both job divisions. Fights with another weaker clone, instead of summoning Tauromacis.
items.shortDescription: The dark clone of Kyrin
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

Geist can be translated to mean ghost in German, and though this Balrog is somewhat transparent, it is strangely not undead.

items.name: Chaos Zakum
items.description: The main body of Chaos Zakum. It is key to attack the body, as attacking the arms will not directly damage the body. However, it will make certain phases easier. While the arms are alive, the body will not directly attack, unless all the arms have been defeated quickly enough. In this case, Zakum will be able to charge up an attack, remaining stagnant for a few seconds, and unleash an explosion that does 99% of your health as damage. Zakum will also be able to summon 2 of each minion during this phase, as well as cast Weapon Cancel. In the last phase of the battle, during the last 20% of Zakum's health, Zakum will gain new abilities. It will be able to burrow into the ground and reappear on top of you, as well as cast Magic Cancel, and cause rocks to fall from the ceiling, which can slow you down and prevent you from using special movement skills, such as Flash Jump. Its main attack during this phase involves a green cube appearing, and the players must hide under the cube to avoid getting instantly killed by its main attack, where it leaps into the air and slams into the ground.
items.shortDescription: The main body of Chaos Zakum
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

In Phase 5, it reappears, but is slightly stronger, and has 25% Defense Rate. It has three attacks: lightning with seal, 1/1 with stun, and a physical attack with slow. Its 1/1-stun attack requires either an autopotter or VERY quick hands to survive.

items.name: Ariel
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Temple of Time, perched precariously over a mirror in front of which the Pink Bean sits. Based on the Goddess herself, it's the strongest of the statues. It can regenerate HP and MP, Dispel, use Weapon and Magic Cancel, cast poison fog, and can use the debuff 'Zombify', which causes the heal skill to deal damage instead of healing. As one might expect from the statue of the Goddess, it's immune to Holy, and strong to Fire, Ice, Dark, and Lightning, making Poison the only possible element that can harm it. It fights along side Pink Bean when activated in form 5 of the battle.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Temple of Time, perched precariously over a mirror in front of which the Pink Bean sits

items.name: Pink Bean
items.description: The boss of the Temple of Time. Pink Bean is a being from another world who was accidentally summoned through the Goddess' mirror by an unidentified man known as Kirstun, who was actually trying to bring back the Black Mage. Don't let its awfully adorable appearance fool you, it can be a tough boss to fight. It can summon Mini Beans, and sports a resistance to Physical, Holy, Fire, Dark, and Ice. This boss has 4 different attacks. The first attack is similar to a Bishop's Genesis, except that the skill fires holy rays upwards and stuns the target. The second attack is a mid ranged big bang like attack that knocks you back a good distance. This boss can also summon a megaphone in which treble clef notes will rain down upon Maplers indicated by a small quaver note on the ground which Seduces. Finally, it can also thump his tail on the ground sending miniature forms of himself leaping up in the air which is a 1/1+seal attack. Can also use Damage Reflect when its HP reaches 90% or below, and also inherits the Zombify skill which causes your party to be hurt instead of healed if healed by a bishop, as well as reduces the effect of healing potions by half. On defeat, Pink Bean cries before hopping through a very dark portal, glaring at the players before it leaves.
items.shortDescription: The boss of the Temple of Time
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

Found in Ludibrium's Clock Tower area.

Only the teddy's body, not the soul, can cause contact damage, and take damage. It has a ranged attack that looks a lot like Magic Claw.

items.name: Snowman
items.description: The offspring of a Human and the Spirit of snow. It has the appearance of a yeti because of that cross but a heart of a human. It was neglected from society and fled to the El Nath mountains, which were created just for it. Usually hidden from everyone, it's been attacking people because the statue of its mother has been destroyed by someone and will attack when it sees someone. Beware, it has an icy mist attack that deals 500 damage every few seconds, which can be deadly to lower levels or melee characters. Another problem is that Snowman has the ability to stun you so you may end up getting stunned within the mist. It also has a projectile, long-ranged snowball attack which is shot from the yeti's mouth. When in danger, Jr. Yetis are called to help, which is indicated by the Hector throwing a snowball onto the ground. Found in El Nath: Sharp Cliff 3.
items.shortDescription: The offspring of a Human and the Spirit of snow

items.description: A stronger Werewolf, able to summon its lower brethren, and sporting a resistance to ice-based attacks at the expense of a fire-based weakness. However, as a Werewolf has high weapon defense, a Lycanthrope has high magic defense. A powerful Fire Arch Mage can handle one without much trouble. Make sure to avoid its claw attack though; use the same methods to avoid it as you would on a Werewolf! Located deep in El Nath, just before the Zombie maps.

items.name: Baby Typhon
items.description: A legendary elemental of air that has yet to hatch. It's aggressive, but it cannot inflict touch damage. Attacks by bursting from the shell and tossing the fragments at you. Once in a while they spawn at Path of Peril alongside fully grown Typhons. Originally, when a Baby Typhon was defeated, it dropped Dark Scrolls and Tobi throwing stars at a very high rate. Now it drops nothing, making it a rather pointless monster. Found at the Crimsonwood Mountain of Masteria.
items.shortDescription: A legendary elemental of air that has yet to hatch

items.name: Hobi
items.description: A monster found west of Leafre, relatively weak. Looks similar to an NPC in the 3rd Job Instructor's home, named Shammos. Humans who use too much dark magic are doomed to turn into Hobi eventually, and that's what happened to Shammos.

items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: I.AM.ROBOT
items.description: This is a robot that was created by Professor Foxwit. After performing its first task, it went haywire and ran off with a map of the MesoGears. It now roams the Krakian Jungle outside of New Leaf City, attacking by shocking you with its wires.
items.shortDescription: This is a robot that was created by Professor Foxwit
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Boomer
items.description: This is an animated bomb monster found in the Krakian Jungle of New Leaf City. They cause a lot of trouble for the city's residents by blowing up construction equipment. Originally, these monsters would infinitely duplicate if hit but not killed, but this was nerfed quickly due to abuse. Thieves can purchase a throwing star from the Cash Shop which resembles a Boomer.
items.shortDescription: This is an animated bomb monster found in the Krakian Jungle of New Leaf City

locations.region: the Krakian Jungle of New Leaf City

items.name: Mighty Maple Eater
items.description: A Nependeath-like flower that grows in Krakian Jungle and MesoGears. It can't move, but it can fire its tongue out of the mouth-like petals. This magic attack is unavoidable and quick. Low level adventurers may want to be careful when around these, as these monsters attack without warning. In the set 3 iTCG update, Mighty Maple Eaters began to drop the rare Jungle Lilies, which are used in the ritual to upgrade Taru weapons. Gryphons are a better hunting option, however, as their pure, high rate spawn raises the odds of finding one.
items.shortDescription: A Nependeath-like flower that grows in Krakian Jungle and MesoGears

items.name: Victor
items.description: Victor was one of the 3 Heliseum Guardians, along with Treglow and Edea. He was well known for being an excellent painter, being a descendant of a long line of talented artists. His paintings had a huge influence on the Nova people. He fell deeply in love with a woman, who was dead. He would paint paintings of her. Then, one day, he learned about a magical canvas that brought art to life. Magnus had Darmoor retrieve the canvas, and then used it to beguile Victor into becoming his servant. Victor, no longer caring about the Novans, took this chance to bring the woman he loved to life. When Victor finished painting her on the canvas, a doll popped out in the form of the woman he loved, destroying Victor's spirit. Pouring salt to that wound, the canvas, which was cursed by Darmoor, absorbed Victor's soul inside the painting and Victor's body became a slave for Magnus. Now, Victor creates new minions (Totem Staves) for Magnus by painting them on the cursed canvas.
items.shortDescription: Victor was one of the 3 Heliseum Guardians, along with Treglow and Edea
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Papulatus Clock
items.description: A steady speed monster. Papulatus occasionally retreats inside its clock, rendering it invincible, which lets it regain HP. However, in this state, it is also unable to hurt you. It is capable of stunning, doing a lightning attack, and reducing your HP/MP to 1, though arguably the most annoying skill it possess is Dispel. Summoned by the defeat of the Time Sphere at the base of the Clock Tower, in a map which requires 30 Star Force to deal full damage. Can only be fought twice a day. Summons High Dark Star and Low Dark Star.

One of these can be seen tricking the Bigfoot boss into attacking players. Watch out for the fire breath, which can stun, and lollipop attack, where its lollipop grows larger and it smashes it on you.

items.name: Treglow
items.description: Treglow is a genius scientist who mastered many areas and excelled in chemistry. He also devoted himself to being a Nova guardian, but wanted to create an artifical creature, which was forbidden by Nova scientists. He didn't care, and wanted to push his boundaries. Magnus noticed his passion for research, and decided to use it against him by pretending to be a merchant and sending a spy to support his research, who built a friendly relationship with him. Magnus managed to obtain rare materials from other worlds for Treglow. Treglow was close to creating an artificial creature, but Magnus sabotaged Treglow's experiment, causing a huge explosion in his lab. He was tearing through his ruined lab, stumbling upon creatures that were created, which they were twisted lumps of stone. Having lost all his sanity, he declared them his great success. His creatures (Reagent Rocks) are now minions for Magnus.
items.shortDescription: Treglow is a genius scientist who mastered many areas and excelled in chemistry
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Bigfoot
items.description: A mysterious Bigfoot, which looks like a reverse yeti, having huge feet but tiny arms. It's being led around by the Leprechaun on its shoulder, so it probably doesn't even realize what harm it's doing! Must be hunted for a quest given by Lita Lawless. It's one of the few high level bosses capable of jumping, and it spawns on several different maps, giving the illusion that it's encountered randomly as it walks through the forest. Be careful if you're exploring the forest, as it could appear at any time!

items.name: Leviathan
items.description: A wyvern boss which resembles Manon somewhat. The word "Leviathan" means "whale" in Modern Hebrew. According to the monster book, it's said that it hatched from a shining silver egg. A loyal minion of Horn Tail, and rules over the wyverns of Dragon Canyon. It has two attacks: one where it breathes ice at you, which can be avoided by getting out of its range, and where it breathes fire which has power knock back. The fire breath attack can be evaded by jumping at the right moment, similar to avoiding an earthquake attack. It spawns in the Valley of Leviathan, a new map added to Leafre. When defeated, it merely closes its eyes and lands on the ground.
items.shortDescription: A wyvern boss which resembles Manon somewhat
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.description: Rarely found in the Shadow Veil Forest's Labyrinth. You will know if this is in the map if the Labyrinth map you're in is red instead of grey. It has the same attacks as the Headless Horseman, but BE CAREFUL, this version of it can deal 50,000 damage. It's best to take it on from afar. When defeated, drops 5 Tynerum Shards, which are used in town.

items.name: Margana
items.description: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest. She's quite dangerous, capable of Zombifying opponents, leaving poison clouds around, and summon the extremely deadly Dark Menhir. since she can also heal her fellow Masters, it would be wise to take her out quickly. She's also immune to Holy, and resistant to Fire, Ice and Lightning, leaving Poison as the sole effective element. Her name is "Anagram" backwards, and may also be a play on the famous magician Morgana.
items.shortDescription: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

locations.region: the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest
locations.map: the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Red Nirg
items.description: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest. Possibly the toughest of all, he has a myriad of formidable abilities, such as summoning 10 Windraiders, Stormbreakers, Nightshadows, or Firebrands, and summoning 5 Crimson Guardians. He has Super Knockback and stun. Be especially careful to watch for the Attack Up buff, which can double his damage. Since he can add a lot of new monsters to the already crowded area, you may want to take him out next. Fortunately, he is neutral to all elements. His name is "Grinder" backwards, and can also be seen as "Red Grin", related to the obnoxious facial expression. Red Nirg may drop the one of its axes (Redner Lv80) when defeated.
items.shortDescription: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

locations.region: the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest
locations.map: the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Rellik
items.description: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest. He can simultaneously inflict 1/1 and poison, which can be quite scary, as well as stun, or cast seal if caught in his lightning elemental Arrow Rain. Watch out for the Super Knockback as well, because you may end up flying into a Dark Menhir or other deadly obstacle. His name is "Killer" backwards, and he certainly can live up to it!
items.shortDescription: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

locations.region: the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

items.name: Hsalf
items.description: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest. He lacks many of the tricky attributes of his cohorts, sticking only with a haste-like skill. Still, don't disregard his contributions, as when combined with the others it can be extremely deadly. Watch out for the Shadow Meso Explosion and other wide-ranged attacks, which just may catch you unaware while preoccupied with other Masters. His name backwards is "Flash".
items.shortDescription: One of the four Twisted Masters found at the end of Crimsonwood Keep Party Quest

items.name: Nagrom
items.description: This is the pirate Twisted Master, found in JapanMS. He is a tricky and annoying one, capable of Dispelling and casting Shadow of Darkness. He can: launch blue sharks at you, a la Sharkwave, spin in the air while firing his gun down, and punch you twice. His name backwards is "Morgan", a reference to Sir Henry Morgan.
items.shortDescription: This is the pirate Twisted Master, found in JapanMS
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Hilla
items.description: The beautiful red-headed boss of Azwan. She used to be a chief shamaness of Azwan until she decided to hand over the city of Azwan to the Black Mage, and received infinite power, youth, and beauty in return. She revived the dead population of Azwan to use as her army. She possesses several skills. She can cast a buff that increases damage you take by 20%. She can also use Damage Reflect, which deals 50,000 damage. She can also slam the ground, knocking any player near her back and shoot fire in front of her to damage the player. She can also heal herself and all enemies around for 315,000 HP, draining your health by 1,500 in the process. She can summon tombstones which deal 50% of your MaxHP and pierces through your iframes so hitting 2 of these can kill you and she can also cast thunder which can stun you. She can also cast a laser blast which will do 50% of your HP if you do not go under the protective shield that appears somewhere in the room. She can also summon Elite Pikeman Skeleknights as backup, and a Deadly Altar, which can pull you toward it until destroyed. Once defeated, she does a quick yell and burns up, and drops Necro Weapons along with other items.
items.shortDescription: The beautiful red-headed boss of Azwan
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Von Bon
items.description: The first boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. It features various skills, such as being able to make the timer speed up or go in reverse. It can also move back to a spot you were standing at previously.
items.shortDescription: The first boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Pierre
items.description: The second boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. He mostly whacks you with his umbrella. When fighting it, you will either have a Blue or Red Hat (it changes throughout the battle). When he is purple, you can freely attack him. He can change his color to Red or Blue, and you must be wearing the opposite hat in order to damage him. If you wear the same color hat and attack him, you will heal him.
items.shortDescription: The second boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Red Pierre
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Blue Pierre
items.description: The second boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. In his blue form, he turns into a fiery twister, chasing after a specific person. When touched by his tornado, you will be sealed. He also leaves a trail of fire that does 3000 damage over time.
items.shortDescription: The second boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Wrathful Crimson Queen
items.description: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe blinds the screen for 5 seconds. She can also take a deep breath and breathe fire in her direction, doing 25,000-50,000 damage to anyone in her path.
items.shortDescription: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Simmering Crimson Queen
items.description: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe seals your abilities for 10 seconds. She can summon the Alluring Mirror, which attracts players and then kills them, healing the Queen. She can also summon Heart Bombs, which explode for 30,000 damage.
items.shortDescription: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Joyous Crimson Queen
items.description: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe reverses your controls for 5 seconds. She can inhale, sucking the player toward you. She can also summon a black puddle, which slows your movement and attack speed and deals damage over time.
items.shortDescription: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Sorrowful Crimson Queen
items.description: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe poisons your character for 10 seconds causing 2000 damage a second. She can cast Zombify and Damage Reflect, which is indicated by the Queen being surrounded by a red bubble.
items.shortDescription: The third boss that you will encounter in the Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.description: A huge fish that is immobile. Summons Bloody Booms from time to time, causes rocks to rain on you from the ceiling, can shoot a beam from it's mouth, and has an attack that can bring your HP and MP to 1. This boss is weak to fire so if you're a White Knight or Paladin, try using fire charge for massive damage. Also, this is the
items.shortDescription: A huge fish that is immobile

items.name: Zakum
items.description: The main body of Zakum. It is key to attack the body, as attacking the arms will not directly damage the body. However, it will make certain phases easier. While the arms are alive, the body will not directly attack, unless all the arms have been defeated before the last 20% of Zakum's health. In this case, Zakum will be able to charge up an attack, remaining stagnant for a few seconds, and unleash an explosion that does 99% of your health as damage. Zakum will also be able to summon 2 of each minion during this phase, as well as cast Weapon Cancel. In the last phase of the battle, during the last 20% of Zakum's health, Zakum will gain new abilities. It will be able to burrow into the ground and reappear on top of you, as well as cast Magic Cancel, and cause rocks to fall from the ceiling, which can slow you down and prevent you from using special movement skills, such as Flash Jump. Its main attack during this phase involves a green cube appearing, and the players must hide under the cube to avoid getting instantly killed by its main attack, where it leaps into the air and slams into the ground.
items.shortDescription: The main body of Zakum
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Magnus
items.description: The traitor to the Nova people, and a minion of Darmoor and the Black Mage, in easy mode. The battle against him can be tough, due to his abilities and surroundings. The map he resides in has a 5 second potion cooldown. There are also Meteors that fall to the ground, green ones taking away 40% of your HP, and the blue and purple meteors deal 90% of your HP. Green meteors are always seen throughout the battle, blue meteors start to rain down at 50% HP, and purple meteors start to rain down at 20% HP. As for Magnus's skills, he can Slow, with the added effect of restricting you from using movement skills such as flash jump and teleport. One attack includes him rushing at you, knocking you back with him. He can also spin around, knocking you back. He has 3 attacks that can 1hko, he can summon green shadows of himself that can descend onto certain spots, he can leap into the air with the Kaiserium and inflame it, dropping directly down, and he can leap into the air and prepare a blast of pink flames that fire diagonally downwards on both sides, forcing you to stay either under him or farther away. When he dies, he burns up.
items.shortDescription: The traitor to the Nova people, and a minion of Darmoor and the Black Mage, in easy mode
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Montrecer
items.description: A car monster that has been in the abandoned Ulu City so long that tree roots have filled its interior and taken over. It can run around by dragging the truck with the forward "limbs." It does possess attacks that can stun the player. It attacks at close range, by digging its roots on the ground and creates a spiky brown cage around you that glows when closed on the player, which stuns the player. It also casts a debuff that can also stun you with a fairly long range. Be careful as this monster can stun lock you if you don't kill it quickly as it can spam the debuff + spiky cage attack combo at you if cornered. This is one of the most annoying monsters in MapleStory.

items.name: Mr. Alli
items.description: An alligator with a bird on its head. It has two different magical attacks: when the player is up close, Mr. Alli blows a strong gust of wind, stunning the attacker. If the player should retreat, he begins to shoot feathers, which cause the bird on its head to attack in an annoying fashion when it strikes someone. Found in Herb Town.

items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Kru
items.description: A low ranking member of Lord Pirate's crew. Wields a sword which it swings at you. It's a malevolent creature, ruthlessly stealing from the poor ginseng farmers of Herb Town and kidnapping Bellflowers to force into fighting. They will slash their sword at any challengers. Found in the Red-Nose Pirate Den of Herb Tow, which is accessed from a Hidden Street in the Old Swamp.
items.shortDescription: A low ranking member of Lord Pirate's crew

items.name: Tae Roon
items.description: A giant Grizzly bear that seems to be the Master of all the fighting Grizzlies and Panda within the region. Found in Mu Lung : Territory of Wandering Bear. Tae Roon uses 2 types of punch attacks against you rather than using a claw like attack used by Pandas and Grizzlies. He can charge an energy ball and fire it at you, which has a somewhat slow charge time but is relatively quick once launched. If he doesn't do this, he punches the ground with great force, knocking you back, and it is unavoidable. When in trouble, he will call Grizzlies to back him up.
items.shortDescription: A giant Grizzly bear that seems to be the Master of all the fighting Grizzlies and Panda within the region

items.name: King Sage Cat
items.description: The leader of the Sage Cats, street cats which gained immense power from the Legendary Being's scroll and the Book Ghost. It spawns in a special area of Mu Lung called "Master Goblin's Forest." King Sage Cat is an exceptionally devious creature, tricking people with poisons and taking over the mind of Tae Roon with a dark spell. His goal is to conquer Mu Lung and defeat the masters inside to prove he is superior. When dealing with him, watch out for the strong gust of wind that he blows out of the bottom of his cloud, knocking you back. He can also cast Weapon Cancel, and raise his defense and attack.
items.shortDescription: The leader of the Sage Cats, street cats which gained immense power from the Legendary Being's scroll and the Book Ghost

items.name: Bamboo Warrior
items.description: A boss found in Herb Town, responsible for stealing herbs. It can raise its Weapon Attack and Weapon Defense. It can also weaken you, preventing you from jumping. It can toss a throwing star at you. It can also create shadow clones of itself to attack you. It can also smash the ground with its bamboo sword.
items.shortDescription: A boss found in Herb Town, responsible for stealing herbs
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Ravana
items.description: This boss monster can be found in the Golden Temple. It can cast lightning spell, which cannot be avoided. It also casts a ray of fire magic attacks. Its number of arms reminds many people of Zakum. It will summon Blue, Red, and Stone Goblins when in trouble to back it up.
items.shortDescription: This boss monster can be found in the Golden Temple
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Female Boss
items.description: This is the boss of choice for many players to take down since you can fight it in all channels. However if your party to take it down contains many leechers, expect yourself to be kill-stealed without warning. She hates any type of light spell, so shes susceptible to Holy and Lightning skills. Commonly referred to as Anego. Located in the map just before the big boss, at the end of Showa Hideout. Known as "Ahma" (which means grandma) in MapleSEA.
items.shortDescription: This is the boss of choice for many players to take down since you can fight it in all channels
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Vellum
items.description: The fourth and final boss of Root Abyss. This boss will burrow into the ground and from time to time, he will pop out (this is indicated by a yellow warning on the ground), knocking back any players in range a good distance. When at 80% HP, he will spit out 3 fireballs in a row, all with the ability to instant kill, no matter how much HP you have. He can also leap from one side to the other, also an instant kill attack. When at about 35% HP, he will start launching poison from its head, which lands on the ground and does 4000 damage a second. When you kill him, you have to wait for him to finish his attacking animation before his death animation begins. When he dies, he burns up and turns to stone. Image is reduced, click for full size. Note, Vellum is even larger than he appears here.
items.shortDescription: The fourth and final boss of Root Abyss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

It has several attacks, such as stomping the ground, rushing into you, and using a special web attack with a huge range, which can trap your character in a cocoon, where you must continuously tap left and right before the cocoon covers you and kills you, and finally it can drop something on the ground that will reduce your HP and MP to 1.

items.name: Tengu
items.description: Tengu is the newest boss of Mushroom Shrine, and is related to the Mushroom Shrine Tales content. Tengu faces you as a last challenge before he helps you save Takeno Kinoko, the sister of Kino Konoko, to test if you are truly worthy of possessing the Wood Element he carries that is required to save her. Tengu, unlike most bosses, cannot be defeated by attacking him directly. Instead, you must rely on special quick time events to defeat him; after specific attacks where he lands on the ground, a purple zone will appear, prompting you to start pressing keys in a certain order (with S representing the Spacebar). Doing this will take out a fraction of Tengu's HP. After you complete the Mushroom Shrine Tales questline, you can fight him again to raise your closeness for the Mushroom Shrine character of your choosing.
items.shortDescription: Tengu is the newest boss of Mushroom Shrine, and is related to the Mushroom Shrine Tales content
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

It also can heal once its HP falls below 80%. It does very large damage with its Magic attacks. He appears to be behind the events surrounding the boss Krexel, due to the fact that he sealed a portal in the Ruins of Krexel.

items.description: The remains of a defeated Chaos Ooparts. It can fly, and has the ability to self-destruct like the Darkstar, summoned by Papulatus, and Bloody Boom, summoned by Pianus, but only at 20,000 HP or lower. If it self-destructs, it deals 10% of your max HP as damage.
items.shortDescription: The remains of a defeated Chaos Ooparts
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Mini Bean
items.description: Summoned by Pink Bean. It can use Cancel Weapon Attack.
items.shortDescription: Summoned by Pink Bean
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Targa Statue
items.description: This boss form is inanimate and will not damage you. However it will transform into the first form if a Fantasy Spirit Doll is offered.
items.shortDescription: This boss form is inanimate and will not damage you
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Furious Targa
items.description: Once the Angry Targa has been killed, this form is summoned. It gains a boost in both its offensive and defensive capabilities proving to be more formidable compared to its previous forms. Other than that, its abilities remain the same. Once this form is killed, two black blobs controlling the boss will fall out. It also drops the Targa Hat, which surpassed Zakum Helm's stats.
items.shortDescription: Once the Angry Targa has been killed, this form is summoned
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Scarlion Statue
items.description: This boss form is inanimate and will not damage you. However it will transform into the first form if a Fantasy Spirit Doll is offered.
items.shortDescription: This boss form is inanimate and will not damage you

items.name: Scarlion Boss
items.description: This is the first form of the Scarlion Boss. It has a wide range of attacks in which it can swipe at you, squash you, summon a black blob driving a mini cart to ram you and chuck its own head at you. It can also cast Cancel Weapon and Magic Attack. It is resistant to ice but weak to fire.
items.shortDescription: This is the first form of the Scarlion Boss
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Angry Scarlion Boss
items.description: Once the first form of the Scarlion Boss is killed, this form is summoned. Its offensive and defensive power have increased compared to the first form, otherwise it retains its old abilities.
items.shortDescription: Once the first form of the Scarlion Boss is killed, this form is summoned
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Furious Scarlion Boss
items.description: Once the Angry Scarlion Boss has been killed, this form is summoned. It gains a boost in both its offensive and defensive capabilities proving to be more formidable compared to its previous forms. Other than that, its abilities remain the same. Once this form is killed, two black blobs controlling the boss will fall out. It also drops the Scarlion Boss Hat, which surpassed Zakum Helm's stats.
items.shortDescription: Once the Angry Scarlion Boss has been killed, this form is summoned
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Diabolic Jiangshi
items.description: The leader of the jiangshi, found at the end of The Bund near Yu Garden. Attacks by firing a projectile at you, spinning to knock you back, and clawing at you.
items.shortDescription: The leader of the jiangshi, found at the end of The Bund near Yu Garden

locations.region: the end of The Bund near Yu Garden
locations.map: the end of The Bund near Yu Garden

items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Gollux: Right Shoulder
items.description: The right shoulder of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return. It can summon Conjured Veilpreys to assist it. It can also cast Damage Reflect that deals 100,000 damage, as well as Avoid Armor, which makes all non-critical attacks miss. It can cause super knockback 30% damage, summon spikes on the ground, causing stun and 30% damage (60% if you are on 2 spikes). It can also lay out its branches across it, and you must climb on top of it to avoid the KO on the platform below branches.
items.shortDescription: The right shoulder of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Gollux: Left Shoulder
items.description: The left shoulder of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return. It can summon Conjured Veilpreys to assist it. It can also cast Damage Reflect that deals 100,000 damage, as well as Avoid Armor, which makes all non-critical attacks miss. It can cause super knockback 30% damage, summon spikes on the ground, causing stun and 30% damage (60% if you are on 2 spikes). It can also lay out its branches across it, and you must climb on top of it to avoid the KO on the platform below branches.
items.shortDescription: The left shoulder of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Gollux: Abdomen
items.description: The abdomen of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return. It can cast Avoid Armor, which makes all non-critical attacks miss. It has attacks that deal 50% damage. Depending on which crystal glows, the attack will hit in that direction. It appears alongside Corrupted Veilstones 1 and 2, which place a death curse you upon coming into contact with the abdomen. There are different mists that can bind the player for few seconds dealing 50% of your maxHp and also a deadly mist that causes the player to be seduced similar to the Crimson Queen's mirror which can't be dispelled and the player will die when coming into contact with the abdomen. It also can summon roots on the floor which have a warning on the floor before coming up, if caught by it, it creates a cocoon and the player must tap left and right fast before the the cocoon closes the player or else it will instantly kill the player. It's even larger than it appears on Strategy Wiki.
items.shortDescription: The abdomen of Gollux, the boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Gollux: Head (Jaw)
items.description: The boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return. Its difficulty depends on how many of its CORE body parts (shoulders and abdomen) you defeat. Once you enter the map, the difficulty is set. It breathes out purple gas dealing 50% damage. It can open its mouth and suck up everything in front of the direction it's facing, causing you to walk toward it and die upon touching the head, similar to the Crimson Queen's mirror (this attack can be disabled when you defeat the abdomen). It can use its hands to KO you (this can be disabled depending on which shoulders you defeat; if you defeat one, only one hand can appear, if you defeat both, neither will appear). It appears alongside Corrupted Veilstones 3 and 4, which place a death curse you upon coming into contact 5 times with the head. It's even larger than it appears on Strategy Wiki.
items.shortDescription: The boss of Tynerum, a spirit corrupted to the point of no return
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Gollux: Head (Eyes)
items.description: The jaw is now destroyed, and the eyes are the center of the attacks now. It can cast a curse on you that reverses your directions, and explodes after about 10 seconds. It has 1 hit KO skills and skills that deal 30%~40% damage. It appears alongside Corrupted Veilstones 3 and 4, which place a death curse you upon coming into contact 5 times with the head.It's even larger than it appears on Strategy Wiki.
items.shortDescription: The jaw is now destroyed, and the eyes are the center of the attacks now
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Gollux: Head (Crystal)
items.description: The eyes are now destroyed, and the crystal is the center of the attacks now. It can cast Avoid Armor, which makes all non-critical attacks miss. It has an attack that deals 30%~40% damage, and can super knockback. It appears alongside Corrupted Veilstones 3 and 4, which place a death curse you upon coming into contact 5 times with the head. Once you defeat the head, you have defeated Gollux. It's even larger than it appears on Strategy Wiki.
items.shortDescription: The eyes are now destroyed, and the crystal is the center of the attacks now
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

Can also make itself immune to Physical/Magical attacks for a short time like Zakum.

It can stomp the ground and breathe out long ranged purple fire, both of which hit 5 times, which can only be avoided by going behind it or far away, making this dangerous for lower HP classes. Ghostwood Stumpy can heal itself and nearby stumps by 8,000,000.
items.shortDescription: The boss monster of Twilight Perion, accessed from Henesys Ruins
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Hilla (Gray Maiden)
items.description: Once Hard Hilla is at half HP, she turns into a gray-headed woman with white eyes. She has the same attacks as her original red hair form. Note, she shares the HP bar, so it's not separate. Upon this transformation, she will fall asleep and summon 10 Azwan Ghosts that you must defeat in order to wake her up.
items.shortDescription: Once Hard Hilla is at half HP, she turns into a gray-headed woman with white eyes
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Shinsoo
items.description: Summoned by Cygnus. It can heal itself and neatby allies by 21,000,000 and can cast a buff that makes Cygnus invulnerable for a few seconds.
items.shortDescription: Summoned by Cygnus
items.farmingReason: More situational for farming than top route picks.

items.name: Cygnus
items.description: The beautiful gray-headed boss of the Knight's Stronghold. She can potential lock, reflect, zombify, and banish you to a different map. She can attack using a dark version of Genesis which can 1hko you or a full map dark genesis which is unavoidable and takes off 90% of your MaxHP, tornadoes which can spin you in the air for a few seconds dealing 1000 damage per second, flaming birds which deal 6000 damage per second when inside the flames, and a purple ball. She summons Chief Knights, Advanced Knights, and Shinsoo. There is a 30 second potion cooldown while in her room. When she is defeated, she will retreat and can drop empress weapons along with other items.
items.shortDescription: The beautiful gray-headed boss of the Knight's Stronghold
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Solomon the Wise
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Time temple. It fights along side Chaos Pink Bean. It has three attacks: lightning with seal, 1/1 with stun, and a physical attack with slow. Its 1/1-stun attack requires either an autopotter or VERY quick hands to survive. Each phase, this form has multiple forms, immediately reviving upon death, and only the last will give experience.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Time temple
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Rex the Wise
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Time temple. It fights along side Chaos Pink Bean when activated at Stage 2, and uses Dispel and Reverse Key. It has three attacks: lightning with darkness, 1/1 with zombify, and a physical attack with poison. Each phase, this form has multiple forms, immediately reviving upon death, and only the last will give experience.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Time temple
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Hugin
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple. The symbol on its chest reveals it to be fire based, making it strong to fire, but with a weakness to Ice. It fights along side Chaos Pink Bean when activated in Stage 3, and can use Seal, Shadow of Darkness, Magic Reflect, Physical Reflect, and all damage reflect. It has two attacks: fire with slow, and physical with stun (markers). Each phase, this form has multiple forms, immediately reviving upon death, and only the last will give experience.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Munin
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple. The symbol on its chest reveals it to be Ice based, making it strong to ice, but with a weakness to Fire. It fights along side Chaos Pink Bean when its activated in Stage 4, and can use Seduce and Darkness. It has two attacks: ice with darkness, and physical with crazy skull. It has three skills: mapban, potion lock, and seduce (left, jump left, jump right). Each phase, this form has multiple forms, immediately reviving upon death, and only the last will give experience.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Ariel
items.description: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple, perched precariously over a mirror in front of which Chaos Pink Bean sits. Based on the Goddess herself, it's the strongest of the statues. The goddess's appearance is similar to the genesis and resurrect animations. It can regenerate HP and MP, Dispel, use Weapon and Magic Cancel, cast poison fog, use Zombify, and cast all Damage Reflect. As one might expect from the statue of the Goddess, it's immune to Holy, and strong to Fire, Ice, and Lightning, making Poison the only possible element that can harm it. It fights along side Pink Bean when its activated in Stage 5 of the battle. This boss has 4 forms times throughout the battle, reviving immediately upon death, and only the last one gives experience.
items.shortDescription: This is a statue found at the top of Time Temple, perched precariously over a mirror in front of which Chaos Pink Bean sits

locations.map: perched precariously over a mirror in front of which Chaos Pink Bean sits

items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Mini Bean
items.description: Summoned by Chaos Pink Bean throughout the battle.
items.shortDescription: Summoned by Chaos Pink Bean throughout the battle
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Pink Bean
items.description: The Chaos version of Pink Bean. Don't let its awfully adorable appearance fool you into thinking this is easy, it can be a very tough boss fight. It can summon Chaos Mini Beans, and sports a resistance to Physical, Holy, Fire, and Ice. This boss has the same attacks as regular Pink Bean, and some new skills. The new skills include : Seduce (separate from the attack), Reverse Key (separate from the attack), Potential Cancel, Teleporting, and it can transform players into Pink Bean, making them unable to use skills. Chaos Pink Bean has 14 forms throughout the battle, immediately reviving upon death. The last form, the only one to give experience, drops Timeless and Loveless Weapons, Chaos Pink Bean Hat and Suit, Black Bean Hat and Suit, Chaos Pink Bean Mark, and various other items.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Pink Bean
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Pierre
items.description: The Chaos version of Pierre. He possess the same attacks and skills as his weaker counterpart. When fighting it, you will either have a Blue or Red Hat (it changes throughout the battle). When he is purple, you can freely attack him. He can change his color to Red or Blue, and you must be wearing the opposite hat in order to damage him. If you wear the same color hat and attack him, you will heal him. Chaos Pierre has the ability to transform into both Blue and Red Pierre at the same time.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Pierre
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Red Pierre
items.description: The Chaos version of Red Pierre. He possess the same attacks and skills as his weaker counterpart. In his red form, he can teleport, and strike with a 1 hit kill attack right afterwards.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Red Pierre
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Blue Pierre
items.description: The Chaos version of Blue Pierre. He possess the same attacks and skills as his weaker counterpart. In his blue form, he turns into a fiery twister, chasing after a specific person. When touched by his tornado, you will be sealed. He also leaves a trail of fire that does 3000 damage over time.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Blue Pierre
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Von Bon
items.description: The Chaos version of Von Bon. It features the same skill as its weaker counterpart, such as being able to make the timer speed up or go in reverse. It can also move back to a spot you were standing at previously. Chaos Von Bon can use the skill Space-Time Collapse, meaning he summons a crack and you must enter the crack and defeat the monster inside before the screen cracks dealing instant KO to everyone inside the dimension, outside the dimension, or in the garden. In addition, Chaos Von Bon has meteorites similar to those of Magnus and uses them with it reaches a certain amount of health.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Von Bon
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Disembodied Von Bon
items.description: The disembodied form of Chaos Von Bon found inside the summoned dimension of Space-Time Collapse. If you do not kill this clone quickly, the screen will crack and every party member will be killed.
items.shortDescription: The disembodied form of Chaos Von Bon found inside the summoned dimension of Space-Time Collapse
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Wrathful Crimson Queen
items.description: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe blinds the screen for 5 seconds. She can also take a deep breath and breathe fire in her direction, doing 80,000 damage which is dispersed evenly to anyone in her path. In addition, she can cast a burn spell dealing 4,000 damage per second, adds on if she keeps recasting it. The only way to remove this burn is to allow Crimson Queen to use her purple fire attack on you.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Simmering Crimson Queen
items.description: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe seals your abilities for 10 seconds. She can summon the Alluring Mirror, which attracts players and then kills them, healing the Queen. She can also summon Heart Bombs, which explode for 30% of your HP, each.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Joyous Crimson Queen
items.description: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe reverses your controls for 5 seconds. She can also inhale, pulling you toward her and killing you if you get too close. She can also summon a black puddle, which slows your movement and attack speed and deals damage over time.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Pinedeer
items.description: A fusion of a pineapple and a deer, formed in Arcane River, a dimension where the Black Mage was able to merge Maple World, Grandis, and FriendStory. Found at Chu Chu Island's Five-Color Hill, an area which requires 100 Arcane Power to deal full damage. Attacks by charging into you.

items.name: Ewenana
items.description: A fusion of a banana and a ewe (a female sheep), formed in Arcane River, a dimension where the Black Mage was able to merge Maple World, Grandis, and FriendStory. Found at Chu Chu Island's Five-Color Hill, an area which requires 100 Arcane Power to deal full damage. Attacks by slamming its front legs into you.

items.name: Antoinette
items.description: A purple version of Pianus found in Twisted Aqua Road, and a stronger version of Pianus that can also summon Bloody Booms and uses same skills as a normal Pianus, however her HP is pretty weak for her level, thus making it the weakest level 210 boss in the game. She also claims she is queen of the seas, as evidenced by her text bubbles. Unlike Pianus, however, Antoinette is an area boss, rather than a lore boss. Their appearance is also different, as Antoinette is purple, and Pianus is orange, much like the Silent Crusade Pianus. Also, instead of an X-shaped scar like Pianus, it has Arkarium's insignia, a menacing eye.
items.shortDescription: A purple version of Pianus found in Twisted Aqua Road, and a stronger version of Pianus that can also summon Bloody Booms and uses same skills as a normal Pianus, however her HP is pretty weak for her level, thus making it the weakest level 210 boss in the game

items.name: Chaos Sorrowful Crimson Queen
items.description: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen. It has four different faces, each with special abilities. She can change her face any time during the battle, to a random face. All of them possess a claw swipe attack with a different status effect. This one's claw swipe poisons your character for 10 seconds causing 2000 damage a second. She can cast Zombify and Damage Reflect, which is indicated by the Queen being surrounded by a red bubble.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of the Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Heart Bomb
items.description: Summoned by the Chaos Simmering Crimson Queen. It can explode, dealing damage equal to 45% of your HP.
items.shortDescription: Summoned by the Chaos Simmering Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Alluring Mirror
items.description: Summoned by the Chaos Simmering Crimson Queen. It can attract players to it and then kill them, healing the Queen so it recommended to kill this as soon as it is summoned. Ignores Status resist that the character has and can not be dispelled.
items.shortDescription: Summoned by the Chaos Simmering Crimson Queen
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Vellum
items.description: The Chaos version of Vellum. It possesses the same abilities as normal Vellum, as well as a new one: it breaths fire across the screen for a few seconds which will 1hko you if you do not go to side of him in time. This boss will burrow into the ground and from time to time, he will pop out (this is indicated by a yellow warning on the ground), knocking back any players in range a good distance. From time to time, he will spit out 3 fireballs in a row, all with the ability to instant kill, no matter how much HP you have. He can also leap from one side to the other, also an instant kill attack. When at about 40% HP, he will start launching poison from its head, which lands on the ground and does 10,000 damage a second. He can appear on one side and breathe fire that pulls you toward it, killing you if you get too close.When you kill him, you have to wait for him to finish his attacking animation before his death animation begins. When he dies, he burns up and turns to stone. Image is reduced, click for full size.
items.shortDescription: The Chaos version of Vellum
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.

items.name: Chaos Vellum's Tail
items.description: Throughout the battle with Chaos Vellum, this will constantly appear to attack you. Avoid getting hit by this, since it will kill you in one hit, no matter your HP. Image is reduced, click for full size.
items.shortDescription: Throughout the battle with Chaos Vellum, this will constantly appear to attack you
items.farmingReason: Built more for challenge and progression than repeat farming.
